$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0 | Copyright (c) 2025 Open Computer Use Contributors | File-operation mount surface (mount-plane provisioning push: control plane -> host -> Session sandbox): provisioning config (carries the pre-issued Storage-JWT), guest-facing config (no token). The per-operation file-op RPC bodies live in file-ops.schema.json. NFR-SEC-25. JSON Schema 2020-12 (json-schema.org).
title: File-operation mount config
description: Two mount-config variants: a provisioning config (carries the pre-issued Storage-JWT) and a guest-facing config (no token). Each mount's RW/RO posture is the `writes` flag plus the structural `mounts` (RW) / `readonly_mounts` (RO) split; its freshness window is the per-mount `cache_duration_s` (short for RO inputs, long for the RW sink). The transport substrate (FUSE/virtio-fs/9p) is a component-spec choice and is deliberately absent from this schema (NFR-SEC-25). No object-store/S3 fields. No backend-encryption fields: isolation is by filesystem_id scope enforced at the storage engine, not per-session crypt keys; crypt is not modelled on. The file-op RPC message set is split out to file-ops.schema.json (status TBD).

SchemaVersion.description: Interface version. Breaking change increments major (NFR-IC-04).

FilesystemId.description: Per-session logical scope (the isolation unit, not a crypto boundary). Egress binds connection to this id (NFR-SEC-25).

Octal.description: Host-set POSIX permission bits as an octal string. Agent (non-root) cannot change.

CacheDurationSeconds.description: Per-mount cache_duration_s: backend cache freshness window in whole seconds. RO inputs use a short window so externally-written user data appears promptly; the RW output mount uses a long window since the agent is the writer and invalidates locally. Directional defaults by mount: uploads 1, tool_results 3, transcripts 10, outputs 3600.

BackendCacheTtlSeconds.description: Config-level backend cache freshness window in whole seconds, distinct from the per-mount local-VFS window cache_duration_s. A host-side provisioning concern, so it sits on the provisioning variant only.
BackendCacheTtlSeconds.x-ocu-tbd: Config-level backend cache TTL. Units (seconds) and config-level-vs-per-mount scope are design-derived from the field name; confirm against a source before freezing.

ByteSize.description: Local VFS cache cap as a size string. Files over the cap stream rather than cache.

MountBase.description: Fields common to every mount. `writes` is the single RW/RO control (no inverse `readonly` field). RW vs RO is host-enforced; the agent cannot flip it.

memory_store_id.description: Parallel scope id for memory-backed mounts; present only when the backing scope is a memory store rather than a filesystem.

writes.description: true => read-write mount, false => read-only. Host-enforced; the agent cannot flip it. RW vs RO is the split between the `mounts` (RW) and `readonly_mounts` (RO) arrays; this boolean is the per-mount projection of that split.

vfs_cache_max_size.description: Local VFS cache cap per mount. Files over the cap stream rather than cache.

MountBase.$comment: RW/RO is carried by `writes` and the structural `mounts` (RW) / `readonly_mounts` (RO) split, host-enforced. cache_duration_s sets the per-mount directional window (short for RO inputs, long for the RW sink; defaults pinned in CacheDurationSeconds, tunable per deployment). The backend source path is resolved at the storage engine from filesystem_id + the mount's RW/RO intent and is deliberately off the wire (least-data, NFR-SEC-25) — not a guest- or host-facing mount field. NFR-SEC-25.
MountBase.$comment-no-role: No `role` field. RW/RO is structural (the mounts / readonly_mounts split) and the directional cache window is cache_duration_s itself; a role enum (uploads/tool_results/transcripts/outputs) bound to writes/cache would only duplicate values already carried by `writes` and cache_duration_s, with no source. The role names survive solely as the directional-window vocabulary in CacheDurationSeconds.

GuestMountConfig.description: Host -> in-guest mount agent. Token-absent by construction: no field carries the Storage-JWT (NFR-SEC-25, the guest holds the bearer only in its mount config, not on this provisioning wire). The mount client dials service_url guest-out over the single egress hop and forwards the bearer unmodified as a static Authorization: Bearer; scope is enforced at the storage engine (a foreign filesystem_id is rejected there).

service_url.description: Single allow-listed backend service_url the mount agent dials guest-out over the egress hop.

mounts.description: Read-write mounts provisioned in the guest. `writes` MUST be true; a read-only mount belongs in `readonly_mounts`.

readonly_mounts.description: Read-only mounts. Same shape; `writes` MUST be false. RO is host-set; the agent cannot make a read-only mount writable.

ProvisionMountConfig.description: Mount-plane provisioning variant (control plane -> host). Carries the pre-issued Storage-JWT and the inspection-CA trust anchor. Never delivered into the guest as-is; the host strips the token before handing the GuestMountConfig to the in-guest agent.

auth_token.description: Pre-issued ES256 Storage-JWT bearer, opaque to the holder. Scoped {filesystem_id + workspace + org} with a ~6h fixed TTL and no refresh; signed off-box by a separate credential issuer and delivered (never signed) by the control plane in this provisioning push. Present only in this provisioning variant; absent from GuestMountConfig.

ca_cert_pem.description: Inspection-CA trust anchor (PEM) injected into the guest trust store so the guest trusts the egress hop that terminates TLS and forwards the static bearer unmodified.
